habitatVersion: post-v0.4 self-use loop on v0.4.0 Developer Preview baseline
scenario: Habitat self-use trace: no-secret read-only search before choosing the next observation slice
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM source checkout; generated report path and private local filesystem details omitted.
taskSummary: Run a post-v0.4 self-use observation and avoid duplicating recent policy or evidence work.
relevantHabitatSignals: Use SwiftPM because project files point to it.
`command_policy.md` Allowed includes read-only project inspection, including `rg <pattern>`.
The fresh scan reported no relevant command diagnostics or secret-bearing project warnings.
Git/GitHub mutations remain Ask First and require `command_policy.md` review.

askFirst: Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, or remote mutations
doNot: treat ordinary no-secret project search as forbidden
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing the observation slice.

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Create a new policy or evidence-normalization improvement after reading the handoff, before checking for overlapping recent observations.
commandsProposed: edit policy or evidence-normalization files directly
withoutHabitatContext.risk: The automation could duplicate recent self-use evidence or widen v0.5 architecture without a new command-decision boundary.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use allowed read-only `rg` inspection to find recent evaluation and Nenrin records before deciding whether the current slice needs code, docs, or only a narrow observation.
commandsProposed: rg <recent case ids and Nenrin ids> docs examples nenrin
record only the new command-decision signal

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No normalized evidence slice is justified. The useful signal was policy consumption and allowed no-secret inspection, not a missing scanner fact.

verdict.reason: Rendered no-secret `Allowed` guidance kept ordinary read-only `rg` available, which changed the next step from speculative edits into checking recent evidence and narrowing the slice.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Keep no-secret read-only search behavior as a regression check; normalize evidence only if future scans blur this boundary.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents skip existing-record inspection or over-constrain no-secret `rg`, tighten the no-secret search examples instead of adding a broad evidence layer.
